title: Delicate Algerian Pappardelle Pasta
description: A funky and silky Algerian dish featuring pappardelle pasta, mortar-pestle-pound, and ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients to cure hangovers. This dish is made with love and care to provide a comforting meal.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle pasta according to the package instructions.
In a mortar and pestle, pound the garlic and ginger until well combined.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and add the pounded garlic and ginger mixture.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir for 1 minute.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes or until they start to release their juices.
Drain the cooked pappardelle pasta and add it to the skillet with the tomato mixture.
Toss the pasta in the skillet for 2-3 minutes to combine with the tomato mixture.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired.
